children.text: Alma is a European Buy Now Pay Later solution serving over 14,000 merchants by offering guaranteed payment facilities to increase their sales and boost customer satisfaction.

excerpt: European BNPL that lets customers pay in installments or later, designed to improve conversion for online and in-store merchants.

content_features.title: BNPL checkout options
content_features.description: Offer pay-later or split payments (commonly 2/3/4 installments) depending on your Alma setup and shopper eligibility.

content_features.title: Online channel support
content_features.description: Designed for e-commerce checkout flows; availability can vary by country and PSP integration.

content_features.title: Guaranteed payout model (provider-led)
content_features.description: BNPL providers typically assume repayment from the shopper while paying the merchant, subject to scheme terms and eligibility checks.

content_features.title: Authorization success & declines visibility
content_features.description: Track acceptance/decline reasons (for example eligibility or risk checks) and monitor success rate by channel, device, and cart size.

content_features.title: Refunds and disputes handling
content_features.description: Support for refunds and dispute flows can depend on your PSP; confirm how refunds, partial refunds, and chargebacks are represented in reporting.

content_features.title: Operational reporting and reconciliation
content_features.description: Reconcile BNPL transactions using consistent order references across your PSP, Alma, and your OMS for fewer support cases.

content_features.title: Regional coverage
content_features.description: Primarily used in Europe; confirm supported shopper/merchant countries before launch and tailor messaging per market.

content_features.title: Multi-currency settlement
content_features.description: Alma supports settlement across multiple currencies depending on partner or acquirer setup.

tip: If you see elevated declines, segment by country, device, and cart value because BNPL eligibility and risk checks often vary across shopper segments.

faq.question: What is Alma?
faq.answer: Alma is a buy now, pay later payment provider that lets merchants offer installment-based checkout options to shoppers.

faq.question: How do customers pay with Alma?
faq.answer: At checkout, customers select Alma and complete a short eligibility/authorization flow. If approved, they pay over time (installments) or later according to the option offered.

faq.question: Do I integrate Alma directly or through my PSP?
faq.answer: Many merchants enable Alma through a PSP/payment platform (for example as a payment method). Whether you need a direct contract or a PSP-only setup depends on your provider arrangement.

faq.question: Where is Alma available?
faq.answer: Alma is primarily focused on European markets. Availability and the specific BNPL options offered can vary by country and partner integration.

faq.question: What is ${providerName}?
faq.answer: ${providerName} is a payment network or payment method used by merchants to accept customer payments.

faq.question: Does Alma support multiple currencies?
faq.answer: Alma is primarily associated with European checkout flows, and available shopper or settlement currencies depend on your PSP and market setup.

seo.seo_description: Alma is a European Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) payment method enabling installment and pay-later options. Learn coverage, integration considerations, and merchant FAQs.
seo.keywords: Alma, BNPL, buy now pay later, installments, pay later, Europe
